title: Cyrtolepis monantha
taxonomy:
Cyrtolepis monantha belongs to the family Fabaceae. 
It is part of the subfamily Faboideae. 
Its genus, Cyrtolepis, is a part of the tribe Dalbergieae. 
Cyrtolepis monantha is a species of flowering plant native to Brazil. 
The species is often included in the genus Dalbergia.

intro: Cyrtolepis monantha is a plant species native to the Amazon region, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtolepis monantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cyrtolepis monantha is typically prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in capsule or tincture form, to make use of its health benefits.
